channel.title: Riddim Yard
channel.description:
Riddim Yard is the home of foundation Jamaican music: Ska, Rocksteady, Deejay, Dub, Roots & Dancehall 
 
email: rickhowe@riddimyard.com.au

item.title: Exclusive Lone Ark pre-releases
item.description:
This week on Riddim Yard, we feature three exclusive pre-release cuts from Spanish roots heavyweight Roberto Sánchez, giving listeners an early taste of what’s coming next on vinyl.

First up was Errol 'Flabba' Holt - Roots Man Skankin’, featuring the legendary bassist from The Roots Radics. One of the most played bass players on the show, Flabba also proves here that he’s got a strong, soulful singing voice to match his legendary status on the instrument.

Next came Robbie Ark - Forward To Zion. Robbie Ark is Roberto Sánchez himself on vocals, and once again he delivers soulful harmonies and beautifully layered arrangements. As always, Rick showed love for Robbie’s voice and his consistently high-level vocal production.

Rounding out the roots selections was Ranking Joe - Marcus Garvey, from one of reggae’s great veteran deejays. Decades into his career, Ranking Joe is still sounding sharp, conscious, and powerful - proof that true foundation artists never lose their touch.

The show also dipped into classic UK roots with 1980s material from Black Roots, representing St Pauls in Bristol - a key hub of Jamaican diaspora culture following the Windrush generation. Their music reflects the deep roots connections between Bristol and Jamaica, and the role reggae played in shaping Black British identity.

On the contemporary tip, there was new dancehall heat from Chronic Law with Glock Fi The Team. Chronic Law was arrested in the United States in late 2025 and remains in custody at the Florida immigration facility sometimes referred to as 'Alligator Alcatraz'. 

International flavours came through with a stop in West Africa, featuring music from Beta Simon out of Côte d'Ivoire, highlighting the long-standing influence of reggae and Rastafari culture across the continent.
item.author: Rick Howe

item.title: Likkle Jordee Spotlight, Phil Pratt Tribute & WYFL Riddim Heat
item.description:
This week on Riddim Yard we spotlight Likkle Jordee, a Native Hawaiian singer with serious reggae sensibilities. We feature the immaculate vibes of his new lovers rock single ‘Moonlight’, then reach back to his earlier work with Ooklah The Moc, selecting cuts from their 2008 album Vaults - a key chapter in modern Hawaiian reggae.

We pay tribute to Phil Pratt, one of the quiet architects of rocksteady and early roots reggae. Through his Sunshot label he crafted some of the darkest, most disciplined rhythms of the era. Dennis Brown’s ‘Let Love In’ remains a certified Riddim Yard favourite - deep, minor-key and built to last - a reminder of Pratt’s subtle but heavyweight influence.

Plus we dive into the WYFL Riddim - the hottest riddim in Jamaica, dominating dancehalls since late 2025. Featuring Mavado with ‘Bad and Bomboclaat Rich’ and Anthony B with ‘Box Ah Rubbaz’, produced by DJ MAC and CrashDummy.
item.author: Rick Howe

item.title: All Female Selection for International Women’s Day
item.description:
This week on Riddim Yard we run an all-female edition of the program ahead of International Women’s Day on Sunday March 8. Jamaican music has often been male-dominated, but this show highlights the women who shaped the sound and continue to push it forward.

Featuring Riddim Yard favourite Lila Iké, alongside deeper cuts from underground heroes like Aura Lewis - the Black Ark backing vocalist whose haunting harmonies helped define Lee “Scratch” Perry’s studio sound. We also reach the UK for lovers rock pioneers Brown Sugar, part of the generation that built a distinctly British reggae voice.

And representing the modern era, the Queen of Dancehall Spice rides the WYFL riddim with her unmistakable energy.
item.author: Rick Howe

item.title: Marcus I meets aDUBta's new Fullness LP
item.description:
This week on Riddim Yard the brand new album from Marcus I meets aDUBta & The Black Oak Roots Allstars - Fullness LP on F-Spot Records, a deep roots project inspired by the golden era of Studio One. Producer and musician aDUBta, a Bavarian drummer turned studio head at Attic Roots Studio, links with Southern France singer Marcus I and collaborators Mauricio-Joran Ceseña & The Black Oak Roots Allstars.

Riddim Yard listeners will know these names well. Marcus I and aDUBta have been in regular rotation on the show ever since their Cut a Wire Showcase (Hornin’ Sounds Records, 2023) - a serious roots set that caught our attention straight away.

Plus more exclusive pre release vinyl 45s from Roberto Sanchez's Lone Ark studio in Spain, including vocals from Roots Radics bassist Errol 'Flabba' Holt, and Studio One singer Earl Sixteen.

And the WYFL riddim is back with Buju Banton's X-Rated cut, and Naarm/Melbourne's own King Ru with Freedom Fighter.
item.author: Rick Howe

item.title: Riddim Yard ain't nothin to dub wit
item.description:
This week on Riddim Yard we open with something different - YAKO440 x GODBODY108’s Dub In Full, a live-instrument dub reinterpretation of golden-era hip hop classics. That live playing gives the set real depth and movement, turning familiar rap foundations into something looser, heavier and more immersive.

Also in the mix, Ras Teo meets Lone Ark on The 40 Children, a heavyweight roots and dub project produced and mixed by Roberto “Lone Ark” Sánchez. The album draws on the story of the Arba Lijoch - forty Armenian orphans adopted by Haile Selassie who became his imperial brass band - threading history, Rastafari and diaspora into a modern roots framework . We also keep Marcus I & aDUBta’s Fulness in rotation, continuing that meditative, spiritually grounded sound.

Elsewhere, new Nigerian energy from 6uff & ODUMODUBLVCK with “10 Minutes” brings a raw contemporary edge into the session. Different scenes, same thread - live dub reinterpretations, modern roots meditation, and current African heat all moving through the one set.
item.author: Rick Howe

item.title: Pugalist's debut album Found Sound
item.description:
New music from Pugilist, finally delivering his debut LP Found Sound after a decade deep in the underground. A collage of downtempo, hip hop and dub influences, held together by a washed-out, dub-wise feel and heavy low-end pressure.

We also tap into the new album Woman from Hempress Sativa and Paolo Baldini DubFiles - a full-circle link-up rooted in one drop, Nyahbinghi and sound system tradition, with features from Tiken Jah Fakoly and Jalifa.

Plus two new dancehall cuts - Masicka with ‘Boasty’, another strong release from one of dancehall’s top artists, and Govana on ‘Mi Seh’, an infectious party tune with good vibes.

And a roots check from Benjamin Zephaniah - Birmingham-born writer, poet and activist, known for bringing reggae and dub into spoken word. We play ‘Wake Up’ (prod by Mad Professor, Ariwa UK 1996).
item.author: Rick Howe

item.title: Jesus Dread - The annual Good Friday tribute to Yabby You
item.description:
The annual Good Friday tribute to Yabby You - the Jesus Dread. The heaviest producers in roots reggae history, responsible for some of the most spiritual and militant sounds ever pressed.

He earned the name Jesus Dread because, despite wearing dreadlocks, he rejected the Rastafari belief in Haile Selassie as the second coming, holding instead that Jesus Christ was the only true messiah. That stance set him apart - and gave him the name that stuck.

We also preview what’s coming for Record Store Day on April 18 via VP Records, including two heavyweight reissues: Congo Ashanti (1979) from The Congos, and a killer instrumental organ set from Jackie Mittoo, mixed at King Tubby's Studio in 1978.

Plus newly remastered tracks landing on streaming via VP from Dennis Brown, Eek-A-Mouse and Garnet Silk.

And new music from Stand High Patrol - ‘Walk Walk Walk’. The Dubadub Musketeerz stretch into new territory, blending roots reggae, UK dub and the house sounds of New York and Chicago. A tale of a nocturnal stroll, a moment suspended in time - the first single from their forthcoming LP Skanking & Jacking.
item.author: Rick Howe

item.title: Thompson Sound pre-releases mixed by Roberto Sanchez
item.description:
A showcase of forthcoming Thompson Sound titles on pre-release vinyl - classic Linval Thompson productions with the Roots Radics, recorded at Channel One Studio in the early 80s.

Remixed from the original master tapes by Roberto Sánchez at his Lone Ark studio in Santander, Spain, bringing new depth to some heavyweight cuts.

Featuring special mixes of Poorman Style (1982) by Barrington Levy, Peaceful Man (1982) from Freddie McGregor, and Perilous Times (1979) by Mystic Eyes, alongside new vocal cuts from Rafeelya and Nadia McAnuff.

Plus the new single ‘Ting Loud’ from Protoje featuring Masicka, taken from the forthcoming album The Art Of Acceptance, out April 17 on Ineffable Records. A killer blend of 80s Augustus "Gussie" Clarke-style production with a modern twist.
item.author: Rick Howe

item.title: Winston Reedy returns
item.description:
Killer new Lovers Rock mixed by Roberto Sanchez ft. Cimarons singer Winston Reedy on the new 45 on Pimienta Records in Spain titled You Got To Believe. 

Plus more forthcoming Thompson Sound titles on pre-release vinyl - classic Linval Thompson productions with the Roots Radics, recorded at Channel One Studio in the early 80s.

Remixed from the original master tapes by Roberto Sánchez at his Lone Ark studio in Santander, Spain, bringing new depth to some heavyweight cuts.
item.author: Rick Howe

item.title: More fire from the Lone Ark
item.description:
Buju Banton sounds recharged on the new single 'Butterflies' - a slick return to the dancehall style that made the Gargamel one of Jamaica’s most feared voices. Classic energy, modern touch, and a reminder that few can ride a riddim like Buju.

Fresh roots pressure comes from Spain’s Bass Lee Music with Benjammin - 'Fire Burn', backed by the BLM Players and mixed by Roberto Sánchez in analogue style. We’ll also check the new Hit Bound 7", 'Ghetto Rock' - another sharp collaboration linked to the A-Lone Ark camp, packed with live instrumentation and heavyweight musicianship.

And for the sound system heads, Roll & Record link with Small Axe on 'Warn Dem Again' - a tough modern cut steeped in 80s digital reggae DNA. Raw bass, stripped-back force, and built to shake boxes properly.
item.author: Rick Howe
